description: Create a single ball with the specified attributes. A model domain must be specified prior to ball creation. While cycling, balls can only be created before cycle point 0 (i.e., when the timestep is calculated).

python_sdk_alternative.workaround: itasca.ball.create(radius, centroid, id=None) - creates single ball with specified parameters

keywords.name: group
keywords.description: The group slot slot is set to s for the newly created ball. If the slot keyword is not specified, then the group name is assigned to the slot with name Default .

keywords.name: id
keywords.description: Ball ID. If not specified, then the next available ID is used. An error is raised if a ball with the specified ID already exists in the system.

keywords.name: position
keywords.description: Ball position that is the origin by default.

keywords.name: radius
keywords.description: Ball radius. If not specified, the ball is created with a radius equal to 1/40th of the minimum domain extent. A value larger-than or equal to 1/4th of the minimum domain extent is not allowed.

keywords.name: position-x
keywords.description: The \(x\) -component of the position that is 0 by default.

keywords.name: position-y
keywords.description: The \(y\) -component of the position that is 0 by default.

keywords.name: position-z
keywords.description: The \(z\) -component of the position that is 0 by default.

examples.description: Create ball at position (1,2,3) with radius 0.5

examples.command: ball create radius 1.0 position-x 5.0 position-y 5.0 id 100
examples.description: Create ball with ID 100 at position (5,5,0) with radius 1.0
Create ball at origin in group 'mygroup'
Create ball at (2,3,4) with default radius (1/40th of domain extent)
